mechanism_narrative: A low and stable federal funds rate indicates an accommodative monetary policy stance, which reduces the cost of capital for businesses and supports equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:low interest rates generally support equity markets
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% but S&P 500 20-day returns are consistently negative or flat (average return < 0.1%) over any 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate above a certain threshold signals a weakening labor market, which typically precedes or accompanies economic downturns, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate earnings decline.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such triggered instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales combined with strong payroll employment growth indicates healthy consumer demand and a strong underlying economy, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 0.5% MoM AND PAYEMS growth > 100k MoM
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending and employment are key economic drivers
failure_condition: If both RSAFS and PAYEMS show strong growth as specified, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative or flat (average return < 0.1%) in more than 40% of triggered periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, strong GDP growth can lead to concerns about an overheating economy and aggressive monetary tightening, which negatively impacts equity valuations and results in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:inflationary pressures can lead to rate hikes
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth is strong during a high inflation regime, but S&P 500 60-day returns are consistently positive (average return > 0.5%) over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts indicates a slowdown in construction activity and broader economic demand, which can signal an impending economic contraction and lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns as future growth prospects dim.
evidence_refs: fact:housing starts are a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If HOUST declines by more than 10% month-over-month, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects increasing manufacturing output and overall economic activity, which typically translates into higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns as investor confidence rises.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth > 0.2% MoM for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows sustained growth for three months, but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative or flat (average return < 0.05%) in more than 30% of triggered periods over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation, as measured by CPI, erodes purchasing power and can force central banks to tighten monetary policy, increasing discount rates and negatively impacting equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL growth > 0.3% MoM for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:inflationary pressures often lead to rate hikes
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL shows accelerating growth and FEDFUNDS subsequently rises, but S&P 500 60-day returns are consistently positive (average return > 0.5%) over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong and consistent job creation, as indicated by non-farm payrolls, signifies a healthy labor market and robust consumer base, which underpins corporate revenues and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS remains above 136800.0, but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 40% of triggered instances over any 6-month period.
